free_text: Two-neutron removal reactions for very neutron-rich
reactions of the Be-11 one-neutron halo.
reactions of the Li-11 one-neutron halo.
a 1141.5-mg/cm**2 Au target and an empty target frame
Detect neutrons and Be-12 in coincidence.
Detect neutrons and He-6 in coincidence.
projectile fragmentation of an O-18 primary beam
impinging at 55 MeV/A onto a 542-mg/cm**2 Be target.
The separation of the secondary beams was made by means
of the doubly achromatic spectrometer LISE.
A 300 mum Si-detector, mounted in front
of the secondary reaction target. After the reaction
target, a delta-E-E telescope, composed of a 300 mum
Si and a 5000 mum Si(Li) detector, was installed for
detectors were placed 3 m down-stream from the target.
There detectors were all 15 cm thick.
A background run with approximately the same number of
ions was performed without the reaction target and with
appropriately reduced beam energy to correct for
reactions that take place in the Si-detector after
